title: Waterman Fund Essay Contest for Emerging Writers
description:
This annual contest is for writers who have not published a major work of fiction or narrative nonfiction on topics of wilderness, wildness or the ethics and ecology of environmental issues. $3,000 for the first-place essay selection and $1,000 for a runner-up. Deadline: Mar 15, 2026.

Categories: Awards and Grants, Journalism & Media

title: AI x Journalism Day 2026
location: South by Southwest, Austin, United States
description: Hosted by Hacks/Hackers in partnership with Poynter DATES:   March 16, 2026  EVENT TYPE:  In-person   About the event Hacks/Hackers, in partnership with Poynter, will come together for AI x Journalism Day 2026 during South by Southwest in Austin, Texas.  The gathering will examine how artificial intelligence is reshaping the creation and distribution of news and information. Programming will explore how public media is using AI, ways to leverage AI for community moderation and accountability for the platforms themselves.  Alex Mahadevan, MediaWise director and Poynter faculty, will join Claire Dannenbaum of Pew Research Center for a panel on building generative AI strategies audiences trust.  The event will feature in-depth discussions and networking opportunities, concluding with a happy hour. Additional programming details will be announced soon.  Please note that space is limited and attendance is subject to host approval. Participants will be admitted on a first-come, first-served basis based on registration.  Request to join   About Hacks/Hackers  Hacks/Hackers is an independent 501(c)(3) non-profit originally founded in 2009 as a meetup for journalists and technologists to improve the quality of the information ecosystem. We are committed to advancing media innovation and safeguarding information integrity by convening events that bring together people from diverse backgrounds, sparking and incubating innovative media projects and scaling media solutions.  The Poynter Foundation is a non-profit 501(c)3. The EIN for the organization is 99-0731998.

title: Travel grants to the first International Conference for the Just Transition Away from Fossil Fuels
description:
Apply now for a grant to travel to Colombia and become part of Clean Energy Wire’s cross-border newsroom reporting on the first International Conference for the Just Transition Away from Fossil Fuels. The summit in Colombia in April 2026 will bring together governments and key stakeholders from business and civil society to map pathways to accelerate a just transition away from oil, coal and gas. Applications from journalists will be accepted until 17 March 2026, 23:59 CET.

Categories: National (U.S.), International, Professional Meetings, Energy & Fuel

title: News Startup Sessions with INN and Tiny News Collective
description:
Join the Institute for Nonprofit News (INN) and Tiny News Collective for News Startup Sessions. The small group sessions will take place with INN’s Courtney Lewis, TNC’s membership director Elaine Díaz Rodríguez, and a few other leaders looking to launch a news operation.
In these sessions, you’ll:

Gain insight into key elements for success
Get answers to your pressing questions
Discover resources to support your journey

Get a head start with INN’s Startup Guide — packed with early tips and resources for planning, launching, and running a nonprofit news organization. See you soon.

title: SPJ Freelance Community Board Meeting
description: SPJ members are welcome to attend. Email spjfreelancecommunity@gmail.com to request a login.

title: DC Environmental Film Festival
description:
The Environmental Film Festival in the Nation’s Capital (DCEFF) annually showcases environmentally themed films. DCEFF is happy to have locally-based reporters attend screenings, but remote journalists can reach out with interview requests for attending guests and filmmakers. Submit a film by Dec 1; cash prizes.

Categories: Mid-Atlantic (DC DE MD PA VA WV), Other Events, Journalism & Media

location: National Press Building, Washington, DC, USA
description: Tickets are on sale for Career Day at the National Press Club, a high-impact event designed for journalists and communicators navigating one of the most challenging job markets our industry has seen in years.

title: SPJ Board of Directors Meeting
description: You are invited to the SPJ Board of Directors Zoom meeting.

title: 2026 Islamic Scholarship Fund
description:
This opportunity supports students in an
Islamic Scholarship Fund-supported field
who will be pursuing their studies as full-time students during the 2026–2027 academic year. The scholarship mobilizes financial resources for students who demonstrate strong leadership potential, excellent communication skills, alignment with ISF’s mission and vision and a commitment to impacting public opinion.
Deadline: Mar 21, 2026.

Categories: National (U.S.), Awards and Grants, Other or All

title: Nomination deadline: Ethics in Journalism Award
description: The Ethics in Journalism Award honors journalists or news organizations that perform in an outstanding ethical manner demonstrating the ideals of the SPJ Code of Ethics. It also honors especially notable efforts to educate the public on principles embodied in the code or hold journalists ethically accountable for their behavior. The Society may present one Ethics in Journalism Award in any given year unless there are no worthy candidates. Nominations are open. Self-nomination is permitted.

title: Truth on the Line: Journalism’s Role in Protecting Democracy
location: Selby Public Library, 1331 First Street, Sarasota, FL, 34236, United States
description: The pressure on journalism has never been greater. Sustained attacks, intimidation, lawsuits, business pressures and defunding efforts threaten the ability of news organizations to do their essential work, from our local communities to the national scene. Yet access to accurate, verified information is fundamental to a healthy democracy, no matter your beliefs.  Join Poynter in Sarasota for an evening of insight, discussion and accountability. The event will begin with a 30-minute reception to connect with peers, followed by a 60-minute panel featuring leading voices in news, fact-checking, AI and media innovation.  This is a must-see event for anyone committed to the future of a free press, ethical reporting and the public’s access to truth.  6:00 – 6:30 p.m. | Reception  6:30 – 7:30 p.m. | Panel Discussion (Scroll down for panelist bios)  7:30 – 8:00 p.m. | Closing & Networking  GET TICKETS   Questions? Please email marketing@poynter.org.  The Poynter Foundation is a nonpartisan 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization working to improve journalism in service to democracy. Proceeds from this fundraising event will power Poynter’s efforts to stoke civil connection and thoughtful expression between journalists and the communities they serve.  The EIN for the organization is 99-0731998.

title: Al Neuharth Free Spirit and Journalism Conference for High School Juniors
description:
High school juniors with an interest in journalism can apply to attend Freedom Forum's annual event, all expenses paid plus a $1,000 scholarship to the college of their choice. The 2026 conference takes place Jun 21-26 in Washington, DC. Apply by Mar 25.

description: SkillsFest26 is a free two-day virtual program featuring hours of hands-on journalism training each day in collaboration with leading journalism organizations. Registration is free and open to all!

title: EVOME Institute Science Journalism Fellowship
description:
The Woodwell Climate Research Center's EVOME Institute is now accepting applications for science journalism fellows. The National Science Foundation-supported Evolving Meta-Ecosystems Institute (EVOME) will provide fellowships for six science journalists to participate in a two-week immersion in interdisciplinary evolutionary and global change biology being conducted at the University of Alaska’s Toolik Field Station in northern Alaska. Fellowships will cover transportation costs to and from the Toolik Field Station through Fairbanks, AK, plus room and board. Deadline: Mar. 27, 2026.

Categories: National (U.S.), Workshops and Fellowships, Biodiversity, Climate Change, Environmental Studies, Other or All, Science

title: Kovler Prize for Trust in Life Science Journalism
description:
This award recognizes reporters who make complex science-related topics digestible for the public. This work is crucial in building trust to support informed healthcare decision-making. Journalists who work in print, video, digital, or broadcast media are eligible. Entries will be accepted through March 27 at 5 p.m. ET.

Categories: National (U.S.), Awards and Grants, Environmental Health, Health, Journalism & Media, Science

title: DC Journalism Job Fair
description: Registration is open for our annual job fair, co-hosted with Georgetown and other journalism affinity groups. Confirmed recruiters include Axios, Politico, DC News Now, Bloomberg, NPR, States Newsroom, Sinclair Broadcast Group, Fox Television Stations, The Falls Church News Press, Report for America, and NBC Universal with more to come. Discounted registration for dues-paying members and free registration for people who have been laid off in the last 12 months. Reach out to DC@nlgja.org if you’ve recently been laid off for help with registration. Secure your spot today.

title: TNC Monthly Collective Call
description:
Our monthly Collective Call is part collective update and part town-hall-style conversation. All members, staff and TNC board members are invited to attend. They take place over Zoom on the first Wednesday of every month at 1 p.m. AKT / 2 p.m. PT / 4 p.m. CT / 5 p.m. ET.
TNC staff share updates about work underway with the Collective. We highlight programs, new resources and decisions that affect members. Founders are invited to share updates about their work. This is a space to surface challenges, ask questions and celebrate progress. Together, we discuss what members need and explore ways TNC can support them. The goal is to learn from one another and stay connected as a community.

Tiny News Collective is inviting you to a scheduled Zoom meeting.

title: Nomination Deadline: David L. Eshelman Award
description: The Outstanding Campus Chapter Adviser Award, initiated in 1978, is presented annually to the person who has made an exceptional contribution to the campus chapter for which he or she serves as SPJ adviser.

title: Nomination Deadline: Distinguished Teaching Award
description: This Distinguished Teaching in Journalism Award, made annually, honors a collegiate journalism educator and recognizes outstanding teaching ability, contributions to journalism, journalism education and contributions toward maintaining the highest standards of the profession.

title: Nomination Deadline: Fellow of the Society Award
description: This recognition honors journalists who have made an extraordinary contribution to the profession. Honorees must agree to attend the SPJ Conference.

title: Nomination Deadline: Historic Sites in Journalism Award
description: The Society’s Historic Sites in Journalism program honors the people and places that have played important roles in American journalistic history. The program dates back to 1942.

title: Nomination Deadline: Howard S. Dubin Outstanding Pro Member Award
description: Annually, the Society salutes individual members who have made significant contributions to their SPJ chapters.

title: Nomination Deadline: Julie Galvan Outstanding Graduate in Journalism Award
description: Each of the Society’s campus chapters are encouraged to select one graduate in journalism who is outstanding in his or her class on the basis of character, service to the community, scholarship, proficiency in practical journalism and significant contributions to their SPJ chapter. From this group, the Society’s leaders will select one graduate who is considered the most outstanding for national recognition.

title: Nomination Deadline: Sunshine Award
description: The Sunshine Award recognizes individuals and groups for making important contributions in the area of open government.

title: Nomination Deadline: Wells Memorial Key
description: Each year, a member who is judged to have served the Society in the most outstanding fashion during the preceding year or over a period of years is presented SPJ’s highest honor for a member, the Wells Memorial Key.

title: From Founder to Leader: What to Consider as Your Organization Evolves
description:
From Founder to Leader: What to Consider as Your Organization Evolves
As your organization grows, your role has to grow with it. What made you a strong founder is not always what will make you an effective leader at the next stage.
This interactive session invites you to pause and reflect on that transition.
We’ll begin by grounding ourselves in what leadership means to each of us, then explore the distinction between “founder mode” and “leader mode.” From there, we’ll work through a set of practical, self-reflective questions designed to help you assess where you are and what may need to shift.
We’ll examine four key areas:

Identity and attachment: What is the line between you and the organization?Who are you without the daily hustle of building? Where might ego, control or fear be shaping decisions?

Strategic clarity: Are you leading from a clear north star, or reacting to urgent demands?

Hard conversations: What are you avoiding that a leader must address?

Sustainable leadership: Are you building a role that can last, or one that depends entirely on your personal overextension?
You’ll also be guided through a personal SWOT analysis tailored to your leadership evolution, with a live example to help you think concretely about strengths, blind spots, risks and opportunities.
We’ll close with Q&A and an invitation: choose one leadership question to sit with this week and complete your own personal SWOT. Those who sign up for a follow-up coaching session can bring their reflection for deeper discussion and accountability.
This session is designed for founders who sense that the next stage requires something different from them and are ready to lead more intentionally, not just work harder.

title: ProJourn Legal Checkup Clinic

title: INN Days 2026
location: Wyndham Grand Pittsburgh Downtown, 600 Commonwealth Pl, Pittsburgh, 15222, United States

INN Days brings together news leaders, technologists and business strategists to identify and solve the nonprofit news field’s toughest challenges. This is an opportunity for decision-makers to dive deep and learn practical strategies for strengthening news as a public good.

title: 2026 Nonprofit News Awards
description:
Celebrate the best in nonprofit news at the 2026 Nonprofit News Awards!
The evening will begin Tuesday, June 16, at 5 p.m. with a reception featuring light hors d’oeuvres and drinks, followed by the awards ceremony from 6 to 8:30 p.m., when the winners will be announced.

title: GlobalFact 2026
description: You’re invited to join the world’s largest fact-checking summit, to be held June 17-19, 2026, in Vilnius, Lithuania.  Presented by the International Fact-Checking Network (IFCN) at the Poynter Institute in partnership with Delfi, Lithuanian National Radio and Television (LRT) and the European Fact-Checking Standards Network (EFCSN), GlobalFact 2026 will empower fact-checkers to uphold the industry’s highest standards of excellence through discussions, training and networking events with globally renowned fact-checking experts.  Laura Zommer, center, sitting on a panel with Patrícia Campos Mello and Jaime Abello, addresses the crowd at GlobalFact 12 in Rio de Janeiro.  About GlobalFact Since 2014, more than 4,000 people have attended GlobalFact in cities around the world including London, Buenos Aires, Madrid, Rome, Cape Town, Oslo, Seoul, Sarajevo and, most recently, Rio de Janeiro. Numerous industry-leading projects and fact-checking advancements have originated through past GlobalFacts, including International Fact-Checking Day, the IFCN Code of Principles and partnerships with major tech companies.  GET TICKETS     What to Expect Networking Events Besides summit-wide social activities and events, GlobalFact allows space to facilitate meaningful connections for attendees looking for professional development, idea generation, mentorship and more. This year’s summit will include dedicated time for both regional networking and topical networking.  Interest-driven Tracks Attendees interested in examining a specific area from various industry perspectives can choose to attend sessions categorized by “topics.” Topics this year include artificial intelligence, information warfare, sustainability, methods innovation and public resilience.  Signatory-only Sessions Verified signatories to the IFCN Code of Principles will have exclusive access to members-only sessions to discuss topics such as working with platforms, protecting independence and database control.  Region-specific Sessions Leaders from Central and Eastern Europe and the surrounding area will discuss the past, present and future of fact-checking in the region and touch on themes such as war propaganda and state-sponsored disinformation.  Expert-led Discussions Attendees will discuss universal challenges facing fact-checkers today with leaders in journalism, academia, misinformation, technology and more.  The agenda for GlobalFact 2026 will be published on register.globalfactconference.org in May.   Sponsor GlobalFact 2026 If you are interested in sponsoring GlobalFact 2026, please contact Angie Drobnic Holan, IFCN director, for more information.   If you’d like to make an immediate impact, please click here. Any donation amount supports Poynter’s mission to strengthen journalism in service to democracy.  Questions? We want to hear from you. Please send your inquiries to globalfact@ifcn.org.  Learn More
